title: ‘Suspicious’ box strapped to tree near City Hall is a state noise monitor for I-81 project
summary: A noise monitoring device installed by NYSDOT contractors on a tree near City Hall was mistaken for a suspicious package, prompting evacuation and emergency response. The device is part of the $2.25 billion I-81 viaduct reconstruction project. While the city was notified of monitoring installations, this specific placement caught officials by surprise, though the device has been identified as safe and will be removed.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Transportation

political_signal.description: Incident reveals coordination gaps between state DOT and city on I-81 project execution despite prior communication; city seeking improved coordination protocols to prevent future misunderstandings.

processing_notes: Dollar amount $70K from unrelated story at bottom of article (stabbing/corrections officer) — excluded from extraction as out-of-scope
Deputy Police Chief Mark Rusin identified as responder; no canonical ID available in source materials
I-81 project context: $2.25 billion reconstruction through 2028 — major capital infrastructure initiative
Transparency theme: Mayor Walsh noted communication occurred but specific tree placement was not pre-coordinated, suggesting documentation gaps
